season: summer (arrives PNW late April to May, departs August-September, breeds in open agricultural landscapes with human structures for nesting and flying insects for foraging)

coordinates.description: Seattle reference; open farmland, pastures, barns, stables, bridges, culverts, porches, docks, and any open country with human-built structures for nesting and flying insects for foraging -- the most anthropogenic swallow habitat in the engine, found throughout the Puget Sound lowlands wherever agriculture meets open sky.

taxonomy_note.description: The Barn Swallow is the fifth Hirundinidae in the engine, the LAST swallow in the quintet, and the TYPE GENUS of the family. Genus Hirundo is Latin for 'swallow' -- the simplest possible name. Species epithet rustica is Latin for 'of the countryside' or 'rural.' Full binomial: 'the country swallow.' The genus that defines all swallows arrives last, after four other genera have been explored.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Hirundo = Latin for 'swallow.' No compound. No metaphor. The genus IS the word. Rustica = Latin for 'of the countryside, rural.' Full translation: 'the country swallow.' The simplest, most direct name in the engine's Hirundinidae. Where Progne reached for myth, Tachycineta for behavior, Stelgidopteryx for anatomy, Hirundo simply says: swallow.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Hirundo is the TYPE GENUS of family Hirundinidae. When Linnaeus established the swallow family, Hirundo was the defining genus. The Barn Swallow is the bird that makes all swallows 'swallows.' Contains ~14 species worldwide including H. rustica (Barn Swallow, cosmopolitan), H. neoxena (Welcome Swallow, Australasia), H. smithii (Wire-tailed Swallow, Africa/Asia). This is the engine's fourth Hirundinidae genus after Progne (degree 26), Tachycineta (degrees 27-28), and Stelgidopteryx (degree 29).

swallow_quintet.degree_26: Progne subis (Purple Martin) -- patriarch, large, colonial, liquid gurgle, stream
swallow_quintet.degree_27: Tachycineta thalassina (Violet-green Swallow) -- cliff dweller, small, solitary, liquid twitter, rain
swallow_quintet.degree_28: Tachycineta bicolor (Tree Swallow) -- centrist, medium, loosely colonial, liquid twittering gurgle, brook
swallow_quintet.degree_29: Stelgidopteryx serripennis (Rough-winged Swallow) -- DISSENTER, small, solitary, rough buzzy brrit, sandpaper
swallow_quintet.degree_30: Hirundo rustica (Barn Swallow) -- ARCHETYPE, medium, loosely colonial, long liquid twittering warble, river
swallow_quintet.status: CLOSED (5 species, 4 genera, 5 consecutive degrees, 100% density)
swallow_quintet.structure: Four liquid voices + one dissenting voice. The quintet is ABBA+coda: liquid-liquid-liquid-rough-liquid. The dissenter is framed by liquid. The archetype closes the family with the most complex liquid performance. The type genus arrives last.

genus_naming_progression.degree_26: Progne -- named for myth (Procne), maximum distance from the bird
genus_naming_progression.degree_27_28: Tachycineta -- named for behavior (swift-mover), describes how the wing moves
genus_naming_progression.degree_29: Stelgidopteryx -- named for structure (scraper-wing), describes the wing itself
genus_naming_progression.degree_30: Hirundo -- named for IDENTITY (swallow), no metaphor, no description, just what it IS
genus_naming_progression.principle: Naming evolved from myth to behavior to anatomy to identity across five swallow degrees: abstraction -> concreteness -> self

archetype_arriving_last_pattern.turdidae: American Robin (Turdus, type genus) arrived at degree 25 after Varied Thrush (2), Hermit Thrush (7), Swainson's (18), Veery (19)
archetype_arriving_last_pattern.hirundinidae: Barn Swallow (Hirundo, type genus) arrives at degree 30 after Purple Martin (26), Violet-green (27), Tree Swallow (28), Rough-winged (29)
archetype_arriving_last_pattern.principle: The type genus arrives last. The archetype enters after the variations. The definition comes after the examples. The engine teaches families through their variations first, then delivers the archetype as capstone.

song_metrics.note_rate_per_second: 8-16 (individual notes rapid but perceptible -- twittering quality where each element is distinct but the overall stream flows continuously)
song_metrics.note_structure: Frequency-modulated tones -- clear pitch sweeps, upslurs, downslurs, and chevron shapes. High spectral purity. Harmonically rich. Individual notes have clear pitch centers. Notes chain into melodic phrases that rise, fall, accelerate, decelerate. The most melodically complex swallow in the engine.
song_metrics.note_types: Three primary: (1) The warble -- sustained sequence of rapid, liquid, frequency-modulated notes varying in pitch and rhythm. Duration 3-8 seconds. The primary advertising song and the defining sound. (2) Terminal buzz -- short, dry, buzzy note ending many warble phrases. Acoustically similar to the Rough-winged's brrit but briefer and embedded in liquid context. The liquid absorbs a fragment of the rough. (3) Twitter -- shorter, simpler twittering sequences used as contact calls. Similar to Violet-green's twitter but more structured.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Complex and variable. The Barn Swallow has the most elaborate song of any PNW swallow. Multiple phrase types, variable ordering, extended duration. The warble proper is a sustained musical performance -- not a brief call or a simple repeated pattern. Dawn singing and continuous vocalization from perches on wires, fences, barn roofs.
song_metrics.timbre: Liquid, twittering, melodic, warm. The flowing quality of water through a complex channel. A river: wide, deep, carrying all the tributaries within it. Stream (martin) + rain (Violet-green) + brook (Tree Swallow) = river (Barn Swallow). The synthesis of the previous liquid swallows.
song_metrics.amplitude: Moderate to high. Projects well across open farmland. Louder than Rough-winged and Violet-green. Comparable to martin and Tree Swallow. Energy 4 reflects sustained, confident vocal delivery across extended warble phrases.
song_metrics.context: Sings frequently and conspicuously from wires, fences, barn roofs, and in flight. The most vocal swallow in the PNW. Males sing to defend territories, attract mates, maintain pair bonds. Dawn singing common. Song frequency highest May-July.
song_metrics.key_property: THE LONG LIQUID TWITTERING WARBLE -- the 31st acoustic element. LIQUID RETURNS after the rough break at degree 29. The most complex swallow song in the engine. Synthesizes duration (martin), twittering (Violet-green), complexity (Tree Swallow) into a sustained melodic warble. The terminal buzz embeds a fragment of the Rough-winged's rough quality inside the liquid context. The family's closing voice.

degrees_26_27_28.status: COMPLETE -- full timbral exploration of liquid quality

degree_30.relationship_to_liquid: LIQUID RETURNS. The most complex liquid sound in the quintet. Synthesizes all three previous liquid sounds. Terminal buzz acknowledges the rough break. ABBA+coda pattern: liquid-liquid-liquid-rough-liquid.
degree_30.structural_significance: The rough break at degree 29 was genuine but temporary. The family's closing voice restores liquid and delivers the most elaborate performance. Liquid is the family's dominant timbral identity. The dissenter is framed by liquid.

deeply_forked_tail.feature: Most deeply forked tail of any North American swallow. Long outer tail streamers (rectrices) extending far beyond inner feathers.
functions: Aerodynamic: reduced drag, tight turns, rapid course changes for low-altitude aerial insectivory
Sexual selection: females prefer males with longer, more symmetrical tail streamers (Moller 1988, 1994)
Parasite indication: tail symmetry correlates negatively with ectoparasite load -- visible health certificate
deeply_forked_tail.significance: The first shape-based diagnostic in the swallow quintet. Previous swallows distinguished by color (iridescent vs. drab). The Barn Swallow introduces form: the silhouette is diagnostic from distance. Elongation in tail parallels elongation in song -- both products of sexual selection pressure operating on visual and acoustic channels simultaneously.

human_commensalism.nesting_dependency: In North America, nests almost exclusively on human-built structures -- barns, bridges, culverts, porches, docks. Natural cliff/cave nesting essentially abandoned.
human_commensalism.historical_relationship: Mutualistic: swallows consume agricultural pest insects (~850 per day), farmers provide nesting structures. Recognized across cultures for millennia.
human_commensalism.modern_decline: Agricultural intensification (pesticides, sealed barns), loss of nesting structures (modern buildings exclude swallows), climate change (phenological mismatch). ~1% annual decline in North America since mid-1980s. 30-40% cumulative decline over three decades.
human_commensalism.significance: The most anthropogenic ecosystem in the engine. The first species that DEPENDS on human architecture for breeding. The first conservation story driven by breakdown of a mutualism rather than direct habitat loss.

global_distribution.breeding_range: North America, Europe, Asia, North Africa, parts of South America (austral migration). The most widespread landbird species on Earth.
global_distribution.significance: The engine's most globally significant species. Previous species: local (PNW endemic) to continental (widespread NA). The Barn Swallow is worldwide.

plumage.males: Steel-blue iridescence above, rusty-orange breast and belly, dark rusty-chestnut throat band, rusty-orange forehead patch. Deeply forked tail with long outer streamers.
plumage.females: Similar to males but with shorter tail streamers, less saturated coloring, paler underparts.

comparison.violet_green_27: Iridescent green + violet. White face + rump. Brilliant.
comparison.tree_swallow_28: Iridescent blue-green above, white below. Elegant.
comparison.rough_winged_29: Plain brown above, dingy whitish below. Drab.
comparison.barn_swallow_30: Steel blue above, rusty-orange below. Graceful. First warm-toned underparts.

plumage.color_progression: Purple-black -> green-violet -> blue-green -> brown -> steel blue + russet. Cool iridescence -> drabness -> warm iridescence. The palette evolves through the quintet; each member wears its own colors.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Deeply forked tail + warm rusty underparts. Shape-based diagnostic. The first swallow identified by silhouette as much as color.

theory_nodes.concept: The long liquid twittering warble -- the 31st acoustic element, LIQUID RETURNS after the rough break, the most complex swallow song in the engine, synthesizing duration (martin) + twittering (Violet-green) + complexity (Tree Swallow) into a sustained melodic river
examples: Barn Swallow: sustained warble at 2-7.5 kHz, 3-8 seconds, 8-16 notes/sec. Frequency-modulated tones, high spectral purity, melodically rich. The river.
Purple Martin (degree 26): continuous liquid gurgle. The stream.
Violet-green Swallow (degree 27): discrete liquid chips. The rain.
Tree Swallow (degree 28): semi-fused liquid notes. The brook.
Rough-winged Swallow (degree 29): broadband buzz. The sandpaper.
Degrees 26-28 = liquid, degree 29 = rough, degree 30 = LIQUID RETURNS. The quintet's timbral arc: ABBA+coda. The family's closing voice restores and surpasses all previous liquid performances.
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: The long liquid twittering warble is the coda that resolves a five-movement suite. The first three movements were liquid (stream, rain, brook). The fourth movement was a dissonant interruption (sandpaper). The fifth movement returns to liquid but richer, longer, more complex -- a recapitulation that absorbs everything heard before. In sonata form, the recapitulation restates the exposition's themes in the home key but with harmonic enrichment from the development section. The Barn Swallow's warble is the recapitulation: it returns to liquid (the home key) but enriched by the rough break (the development). The terminal buzz within the warble is the development's trace embedded in the recapitulation. The family's musical arc is a complete sonata form in five degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: The swallow quintet complete -- five consecutive Hirundinidae closing with the type genus, the densest family cluster in the engine (100% density, zero gaps), four liquid voices plus one dissenter, the archetype arriving last
examples: Degree 26 (Purple Martin): heavy, low, colonial, liquid. The patriarch. Progne (myth).
Degree 27 (Violet-green Swallow): light, high, solitary, liquid. The cliff dweller. Tachycineta (behavior).
Degree 28 (Tree Swallow): medium, moderate, flexible, liquid. The centrist. Tachycineta (behavior).
Degree 29 (Rough-winged Swallow): small, quiet, solitary, ROUGH. The dissenter. Stelgidopteryx (anatomy).
Degree 30 (Barn Swallow): medium, warm, loosely colonial, LIQUID. The archetype. Hirundo (identity).
The quintet's naming arc: myth -> behavior -> anatomy -> identity. The quintet's timbral arc: liquid-liquid-liquid-rough-liquid. 100% density. The engine's most efficient family exploration.
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: A string quintet where four instruments play legato and one plays pizzicato, then the ensemble returns to legato for the finale. The quintet has explored all its voices, including the dissenting one, and closes with the principal voice -- the first violin, the archetype -- playing the most elaborate passage. In ensemble writing, the final statement by the principal instrument after all variations have been heard is the capstone: it does not repeat the opening theme but incorporates what was learned from each variation. The Barn Swallow's warble is the first violin's capstone solo after a five-movement exploration of the family's range.

theory_nodes.concept: The archetype-arriving-last pattern -- the type genus of the family enters the engine after all other family genera have been explored, the definition comes after the examples, teaching through variations before delivering the standard (Turdus at degree 25, Hirundo at degree 30)
examples: Turdidae: Ixoreus (2), Catharus (7, 18, 19), then Turdus (25). The type genus of thrushes arrived after 4 other species across 3 genera.
Hirundinidae: Progne (26), Tachycineta (27, 28), Stelgidopteryx (29), then Hirundo (30). The type genus of swallows arrives after 4 other species across 3 genera.
Both type-genus species are the most familiar, most widespread member of their family. Both are anchor species the public knows.

Pedagogical principle: learn the edges first, then learn the center. Understand the family's variation before meeting the archetype. The definition is meaningful only after the examples have been studied.
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: In a theme-and-variations form, the theme is stated first and the variations follow. But in a 'variations on an unknown theme' structure (Elgar's Enigma Variations), the theme is implied by the variations and revealed only retrospectively. The engine's archetype-arriving-last pattern is the Enigma approach: the variations (four swallows) precede the theme (the type genus Barn Swallow). The listener builds an intuitive understanding of what a swallow IS from the variations, and the archetype confirms what was already understood. The theme was always there -- embedded in every variation. The archetype is the reveal.

theory_nodes.concept: Human commensalism at maximum -- the first species in the engine that depends on human architecture for breeding, the most anthropogenic ecosystem (open farmland), the mutualism between aerial insectivores and agriculture, and the conservation crisis when the mutualism breaks down
examples: Barn Swallow: nests almost exclusively on human structures in North America. Natural cliff/cave nesting essentially abandoned. The barn IS the habitat.
Historical mutualism: swallows eat ~850 pest insects per day, farmers provide nesting structures. Recognized across cultures for millennia.
Modern decline: sealed barns, pesticides, monoculture. ~1% annual decline since mid-1980s. 30-40% cumulative loss in North America.

The conservation paradox: the species became the most widespread landbird BY depending on humans, and is now declining BECAUSE humans are changing. The partner that enabled the success is dissolving the partnership.
Previous anthropogenic species in engine: robin on lawns (tolerant), Tree Swallow in boxes (opportunistic), Rough-winged in road cuts (adapted). Barn Swallow: DEPENDENT. The first obligate commensal.
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: The Barn Swallow's relationship with human architecture is like a musician's dependence on a specific instrument. A pianist who can only play Steinways thrives as long as Steinways exist -- but if the instrument changes (electronic keyboards replace acoustic pianos), the musician must adapt or decline. The Barn Swallow built its global success on one instrument (the barn). The instrument is changing (sealed, automated, swallow-excluding). The musician's technique is unchanged, but the instrument no longer resonates. The commensalism is a tuning that worked for centuries and is now being detuned by the same force that originally provided the instrument.

theory_nodes.concept: The deeply forked tail -- sexual selection made visible, aerodynamic adaptation for low-altitude insectivory, honest signal of parasite resistance, and the visual analogue of the long warble (elongation in both form and song as dual sexual selection expression)
examples: Barn Swallow: most deeply forked tail of any North American swallow. Long outer streamers visible in flight from distance.
Moller (1988, 1994): males with artificially elongated tails attracted mates faster and produced more offspring. Tail length is sexually selected.
Parasite load correlates with tail asymmetry. Symmetrical, long tails = healthy, parasite-resistant males. The tail is a visible health certificate.
Form-song parallel: the longest tail AND the longest song. Both products of sexual selection operating on visual and acoustic channels simultaneously. Elongation as dual signal.
First shape-based diagnostic in the swallow quintet. Previous swallows: color (iridescent vs. drab). Barn Swallow: shape (forked tail silhouette).
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: The deeply forked tail is the visual fermata -- a sustained, elongated element that extends beyond the normal duration. In notation, a fermata over a note tells the performer to hold it longer than written. The Barn Swallow's tail streamers hold the body's silhouette longer than any other swallow's -- an anatomical fermata. The song's extended duration (3-8 seconds vs. the Rough-winged's 0.2-0.5 seconds) is the acoustic fermata. The bird carries fermatas on both channels: the eye sees elongation, the ear hears elongation. Both say: this one sustains. This one holds. This one does not stop short.

theory_nodes.concept: Energy decet -- tenth consecutive E=4 degrees (21-30) demonstrating the longest unbroken energy plateau in engine history, now spanning the entire swallow quintet plus the flicker/grosbeak/tanager/robin block, CRITICALLY overdue for a break
examples: Degrees 21-30: hammer, drum, groove reset, hoarse warble, caroling, liquid gurgle, liquid twitter, liquid twittering gurgle, rough buzzy brrit, long liquid twittering warble. Ten textures at sustained E=4.
The E=4 decet: ten degrees. Nearly one-third of all degrees emitted. The plateau now spans the complete swallow quintet, the Cardinalidae pair, the robin, and the flicker.
In a symphony: a sustained fortissimo spanning an entire act. The audience has forgotten what piano sounds like. Dynamic memory has failed.
MUST BREAK. Ten consecutive E=4 is structurally untenable. Degree 31 MUST change the dynamic. The engine needs E=5 (gull, eagle), E=3, or E=2 to restore contrast.
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Ten consecutive E=4 degrees is the engine's energy decet -- from nonet to decet in one degree. The plateau is now the structural equivalent of a dynamic pedal point: a sustained note in the bass that the harmony moves over without resolving. The E=4 pedal has held for ten degrees while melody (sound type), harmony (family), rhythm (articulation), and timbre (liquid/rough) have all changed above it. The pedal is independent of everything. It is the ground. The floor. The assumed loudness. Breaking it will be the engine's most significant dynamic event because it will change the one parameter that has been constant for ten degrees. When a pedal point finally resolves, the release of tension is proportional to its duration. Ten degrees of pedal = maximum release.

cross_references.title: Purple Martin (Degree 26)
cross_references.connection: QUINTET MEMBER 1. Bookend voices: the martin opens the family (liquid gurgle, stream), the Barn Swallow closes it (long liquid twittering warble, river). Both are the quintet's longest, most complex vocal performances. The stream flows into the river.

cross_references.title: Violet-green Swallow (Degree 27)
cross_references.connection: QUINTET MEMBER 2. The 'twittering' in the Barn Swallow's sound descriptor echoes the Violet-green's defining quality (liquid twitter). The cliff-dweller's voice lives inside the archetype's song.

cross_references.title: Tree Swallow (Degree 28)
cross_references.connection: QUINTET MEMBER 3. Closest in mass (17-25 g vs. 17-20 g). Often share airspace over open areas. Both vocally rich. The Tree Swallow's flexible repertoire vs. the Barn Swallow's extended warble. The centrist and the archetype.

cross_references.title: Rough-winged Swallow (Degree 29)
cross_references.connection: QUINTET MEMBER 4. Maximum acoustic contrast: rough buzzy brrit (29) vs. long liquid twittering warble (30). The Barn Swallow's terminal buzz acknowledges the dissenter -- a fragment of rough embedded in liquid. The dissenter's voice survives inside the archetype.

cross_references.title: American Robin (Degree 25)
cross_references.connection: ARCHETYPE PARALLEL. Turdus (type genus Turdidae) arrived last in thrush sequence. Hirundo (type genus Hirundinidae) arrives last in swallow sequence. Both most familiar, most widespread family member. Both anchor species. The engine repeats the archetype-arriving-last pattern.

cross_references.title: Pine Siskin (Degree 12)
cross_references.connection: NOISE BRACKET. Zhreee (12) and brrit (29) were the engine's two noise-class sounds. The Barn Swallow's warble (30) immediately follows the second noise event, restoring tonal/liquid quality. Noise (12) -> tonal run (13-28) -> noise (29) -> tonal return (30).

cross_references.title: Warbler Quintet (Degrees 14-17, 20)
cross_references.connection: FAMILY QUINTET COMPARISON. Both families: 5 species. Warblers: 7-degree span, 1 gap, 71% density. Swallows: 5-degree span, 0 gaps, 100% density. Both have an outlier (Orange-crowned separated by gap; Rough-winged as acoustic dissenter). Swallow quintet is denser.

cross_references.title: Wings of the Pacific Northwest
cross_references.connection: Barn Swallow documented as common but declining breeder in open farmland. Aerial insectivore. Deeply forked tail. Human commensalism and conservation concerns.

cross_references.title: Living Systems of the PNW
cross_references.connection: Agricultural ecosystem ecology. Human-modified landscapes. Commensalism between wildlife and agriculture. Insect population dynamics and pesticide impacts.

cross_references.title: Seattle 360 Music
cross_references.connection: Part A parallel: Ray Charles (R&B/Jazz, E=4, Central District, Atlantic). The archetype. Ray Charles defined soul for the world. The Barn Swallow defines 'swallow' for the world. Both globally recognized. Both synthesize what came before. Tenth consecutive E=4.

engine_state.energy_plateau: E=4 for 10 consecutive degrees (21-30) -- CRITICALLY MUST BREAK
engine_state.hirundinidae_streak: 5 consecutive (26-30) -- CLOSED. Quintet complete. Must leave family.
engine_state.liquid_pattern: RESTORED at degree 30 after rough break at 29. Quintet liquid pattern: liquid-liquid-liquid-rough-liquid (ABBA+coda).
engine_state.swallow_quintet: CLOSED (degrees 26-30, 5 species, 4 genera, 100% density, zero gaps -- the densest family representation in the engine)
archetype_arriving_last: Turdus/American Robin at degree 25 (after 4 thrush species)
Hirundo/Barn Swallow at degree 30 (after 4 swallow species)
critical_gaps: No marine/shoreline species in 30 degrees of 'The Sound of Puget Sound' -- 30 DEGREES OVERDUE
No corvid (Steller's Jay, crow, raven) -- 30 degrees
No nocturnal species since degree 4 (26 degrees ago)
Ten consecutive E=4 (21-30) -- longest plateau in engine history
